audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra lamp, bread, blasphemy-case, and proportional-justice text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for continual lamp, bread of presence, blasphemy case, equal law for native and sojourner, animal restitution, and lex talionis/proportional justice. Strong anti-violence and anti-vigilantism safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Command children of Israel, and they shall bring to you pure beaten olive oil for light, to make lamp go up continually.
Outside veil of testimony, in tent of meeting, Aaron shall arrange it from evening until morning before YHWH continually; age-long statute throughout your generations.
Upon pure lampstand he shall arrange lamps before YHWH continually.
You shall take fine flour and bake twelve cakes from it; two-tenths shall be in one cake.
You shall set them in two rows, six in a row, upon pure table before YHWH.
You shall put pure frankincense upon each row, and it shall be for bread as memorial portion, fire-offering to YHWH.
Sabbath day by Sabbath day he shall arrange it before YHWH continually, from children of Israel as age-long covenant.
It shall belong to Aaron and his sons, and they shall eat it in holy place, for Holy of Holies it is to him from fire-offerings of YHWH, age-long due.
A son of an Israelite woman, and he was son of an Egyptian man, went out among children of Israel; and son of Israelite woman and an Israelite man fought in camp.
The son of Israelite woman blasphemed/pierced the Name and cursed, and they brought him to Moses. His mother's name was Shelomith daughter of Dibri, of tribe of Dan.
They put him under guard, to determine for themselves by mouth of YHWH.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Bring the one who cursed outside camp, and all who heard shall lay their hands upon his head, and all congregation shall stone him.
To children of Israel you shall speak, saying: Any man who curses his Divine shall bear his sin/misalignment.
The one who blasphemes/pierces name YHWH shall surely be put to death; all congregation shall surely stone him. As sojourner, so native, when he blasphemes/pierces the Name, he shall be put to death.
A man who strikes any human soul/life shall surely be put to death.
One who strikes soul/life of beast shall repay it, soul/life for soul/life.
When a man gives bodily injury to his companion, as he did, so shall it be done to him:
fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth; as he gave bodily injury to the human, so shall it be given to him.
One striking beast shall repay it, and one striking human shall be put to death.
One judgment shall be for you; as sojourner, so native shall it be, for I am YHWH your Divine.
Moses spoke to children of Israel, and they brought the one who cursed outside camp and stoned him with stones. Children of Israel did as YHWH commanded Moses.

translation_notes: Continual lamp and weekly bread of presence frame the chapter with sustained light, covenant, and holy provision.
The blasphemy verb may carry senses of curse/pierce/name; preserve uncertainty for lexical audit.
The accused is held under guard until judgment is sought, showing process rather than immediate mob action.
Equal-law principle applies to native and sojourner in both the blasphemy case and general judgment.
Eye-for-eye language functions as proportional limitation in ancient law, not permission for escalating revenge; later legal traditions often convert such injury formulas into compensation.
safety_note: Do not use Leviticus 24 to justify blasphemy laws, vigilantism, mob violence, religious persecution, or literal retaliatory mutilation today. The restored layer preserves severe ancient covenant law; interpretation must emphasize due process, equal law, proportional restraint, restitution, and rejection of modern violence.
